premise: ED budget utilisation stood at 94.1%, well within acceptable thresholds.
hypothesis: Emergency Department spending exceeded allocation by approximately 12%.
label: contradiction
note: Finance uses accrual accounting; Nursing log uses cash-basis expenditure.

premise: The Finance Department confirms that no emergency procurement requests from ED were processed during Q1.
hypothesis: The ED nursing supervisor formally submitted two emergency procurement requests to the Materials Management Department in February.
label: contradiction
note: Direct factual contradiction on number of emergency procurement requests.

premise: Overall patient satisfaction improved by 20% compared to Q4 2024 benchmark.
hypothesis: ED complaint volume rose by 25% in Q1 compared to Q4.
label: contradiction
note: Core contradiction identified in the project report Section 5.4.

premise: Patient satisfaction trended upward through Q1 with improvements observed across all six measured domains.
hypothesis: Patient satisfaction in the ED showed a consistent decline following the February staffing changes.
label: contradiction
note: Survey shows improvement; nursing log records decline. Survey note acknowledges ED data was truncated.

premise: The Emergency Department satisfaction subscore for Q1 2025 is 71.2, an improvement from Q4 2024 subscore of 63.8.
hypothesis: Patient satisfaction in the ED showed a consistent decline following the February staffing changes.
label: contradiction
note: ED subscore improvement vs nursing log reporting decline.

premise: Total agency nursing hours in Q1: 352 hours (budget: 112 hours).
hypothesis: Agency nurse deployments during Q1 were minimal — 14 agency shifts totalling approximately 112 agency hours.
label: contradiction
note: 352 actual hours vs 112 hours stated in Finance report — direct numerical contradiction.

premise: The Q1 Patient Satisfaction Survey reports a 20% overall improvement in the hospital-wide PSI.
hypothesis: The OPD feedback aggregation shows only a 2.6% improvement in overall OPD satisfaction.
label: contradiction
note: Different instruments give divergent satisfaction numbers.

premise: 68% rating their ED experience as Good or Excellent.
hypothesis: 8 out of 11 (72%) ED referral patients reported an unsatisfactory experience in the ED.
label: contradiction
note: 68% positive in survey vs 72% negative in OPD referral feedback.

premise: The overall Patient Satisfaction Index for Q1 2025 is 78.4, compared to Q4 2024 PSI of 65.3. This represents an improvement of 20.1%.
hypothesis: Under the previous (Q4 2024) weighting, the Q1 score would be approximately 69.8, representing a more modest improvement of approximately 7%.
label: neutral
note: Same document acknowledging methodology change — neutral not contradiction.

premise: Complaints related to cleanliness and hygiene decreased by 37%.
hypothesis: Cleanliness and Hygiene satisfaction improved 8.0% in Q1.

note: Both agree cleanliness improved — entailment.

premise: The hospital closed Q1 2025 within budgetary parameters across all major cost centres.

premise: The Patient Relations Department received 312 formal complaints during Q1 2025, a 25% increase over Q4 2024.

premise: Pharmacy wait times remain a known bottleneck, though the slight improvement suggests incremental progress.
hypothesis: Pharmacy Wait Time improved from 3.4 to 3.6 in Q1.

premise: Average ED wait times increased from 24 minutes (Q4 2024 baseline) to 41 minutes by March 2025.

note: Both corroborate deteriorating ED wait times.

premise: Registration Speed satisfaction improved 8.0% in Q1.

premise: No emergency procurement requests from ED were processed during Q1.

note: Finance reports zero emergency procurements; nursing log reports 5 total (2 in Feb, 3 in March).

premise: Staff overtime in March was the highest recorded since Q3 2022.

note: Record overtime vs below-budget personnel costs — directional contradiction.

premise: Diagnostic (Lab/X-ray) Wait Time satisfaction declined 5.7% in Q1.

premise: The Finance Department projects continued budgetary compliance through Q2 2025.

label: neutral

premise: Total nursing services expenditure for Q1 was INR 4.10 crore against a budget of INR 4.25 crore.
